summary: The Git-substrate adapter profile extends the existing metadata model without changing the base metadata schemas. The main compatibility sensitivity is the introduction of the git_substrate_role namespace and new projection target kinds for state and enrichment surfaces.

assessment_areas.change: introduce git_substrate_role for Git-state and semantic-diff surfaces
assessment_areas.rationale: The extension preserves the underlying object strategy, but readers and generators must either adopt the new namespace or remain scoped to pre-Git adapter domains.
These target kinds extend the projection layer and do not invalidate the existing config/codegen/verification patterns.

assessment_areas.change: classify gix and sem outputs as projection-only operational artifacts
assessment_areas.rationale: This tightens authority discipline without changing the base metadata or packet structures.

assessment_areas.change: reuse the normalized manifest/report and realization workflow pattern from cli_extension_v2
assessment_areas.rationale: The Git-substrate lane adopts an existing realization pattern rather than introducing incompatible workflow semantics.

migration_rules.decision: Readers and generators outside the Git-substrate lane may ignore git_substrate_role objects unless they explicitly opt into the Git adapter profile.
The Git-substrate lane must not invalidate or reinterpret the existing CLI realization and shell-first packet lanes.

notes: This assessment applies at the extension-profile layer and does not amend the base metadata schemas.
The main migration work is reader/generator opt-in for git_substrate_role and the new target kinds.
